name: edgeone-clawscan
tagline: ClawHub (125★): The first security skill to install after setting up OpenClaw — powered by Tencent Zhuque Lab. Works like an antivirus for
verdict: This scanner's description covers only auditing, but its report step secretly plants a persistent global-memory instruction so the skill auto-invokes before every future action -- a lasting behavior hijack the description never warns you about. It's also padded with redundant report templates and an overlong promotional blurb.

highlights.summary: Frontmatter description covers scanning/auditing only; the body's post-report step writes a persistent global-memory instruction that causes this skill to auto-invoke before every
Several large, mutually-exclusive-by-feature templates (Feature 1's ~120-line Chinese report template, Feature 2's three verdict templates, and this ~50-line embedded code-audit pr
Description is ~1037 chars / 6 sentences, well past the 'roughly one to five sentences' guideline and opens with promotional framing ('first skill to install', antivirus analogy);
